examples: HAMILTON E HOLMES STATION -> Hamilton E Holmes Station
WEST LAKE STATION -> West Lake Station
WEST LAKE STATION -> West Lake Station
fixes.label: Recased shouting stop names

findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 2836 of 7052 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 7028 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

findings.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
findings.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and any elevators, with a level for each.
findings.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt.
findings.why: Trip planners can't guide riders through the station, and there is no step-free route information for wheelchair users.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 60% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (60% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Delete the empty calendars or set their service days.
findings.what: Some service calendars have no days of the week switched on.
findings.why: Trips tied to these calendars never run; they are dead data that can mask real schedule problems.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'route_long_name_contains_short_name' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Route long name contains short name (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already expired.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stops_match_shape_out_of_order' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stops match shape out of order (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
findings.what: A recommended GTFS file (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
findings.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unused_station' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unused station (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 9 kinds of issue across 13551 instances (0 error, 13549 warning, 2 informational).

findings.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
findings.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
findings.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
findings.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 58 days.

findings.fix: Check the service alerts endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The service alerts realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
findings.what: 119 of 236 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
findings.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 6 times: 2 of 3 feeds healthy; 49.6% of scheduled trips had live predictions; 97.0% of vehicles on their route; predictions ran a median of 0s behind schedule.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 58 days.
States wheelchair access on 60% of stops and 100% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. States wheelchair access on 60% of stops and 100% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: If you report to the National Transit Database, your GTFS agency_id should be your five-digit NTD ID by report year 2026 (2025 for full reporters). We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this one is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 58 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 18 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (2, 20, 23, 26, 39, and more).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 6786 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("HOWELL MILL RD @ KIPLING DR", "N MAIN ST @ WINTHROPE PARK DR", "N MAIN ST @ 379", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

recommendations.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
recommendations.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and elevators, and levels.txt for each floor, so the step-free route is described.
recommendations.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt and levels.txt.
recommendations.why: Without pathways and levels, a wheelchair user can't tell whether a step-free route exists inside the station, and trip planners can't route anyone through it.

top_fixes.fix: Check the service alerts endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
top_fixes.what: The service alerts realtime feed failed during sampling.
top_fixes.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

top_fixes.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
top_fixes.what: 119 of 236 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
top_fixes.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

top_fixes.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
top_fixes.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
top_fixes.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
top_fixes.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.
